zotero.abstractNote: Learning disentangled representations of data is a fundamental problem in machine learning. Most existing approaches rely on the assumption that the underlying factors of variation are independent. However, in many real-world scenarios, factors are correlated. In this work, we propose a novel framework for disentanglement that explicitly accounts for correlations between factors. We introduce the Hausdorff Factorized Support (HFS) loss, which encourages the model to learn a representation where the support of the joint distribution of factors is factorized. We demonstrate that our approach effectively disentangles correlated factors on several synthetic and real-world datasets, outperforming existing state-of-the-art methods.
zotero.conferenceName: The Eleventh International Conference on Learning Representations (ICLR)
zotero.proceedingsTitle: The Eleventh International Conference on Learning Representations (ICLR)
